summary: A 2D physics-based artillery game where players control units inside a moving castle to shoot projectiles at an enemy castle, aiming to deplete its health.
objective: Destroy the enemy castle by reducing its health to 0% before your own castle is destroyed.

controls.observation: Drag from a unit to aim, creating a dotted trajectory line.
Release drag to fire the projectile.

layout.observation: Top: Blue and Red health bars with percentages. Center: Two castles on a terrain. Inside castles: Units on different floors.

entities.name: Blue Castle
entities.visible_behavior: Moves forward, takes damage, houses units.

entities.name: Red Castle
entities.visible_behavior: Moves forward, takes damage, houses units, fires projectiles.

entities.name: Skeleton Unit
entities.visible_behavior: Aimed and fired by player, shoots multiple projectiles.

entities.name: Projectile
entities.visible_behavior: Travels in an arc, explodes on impact.

timeline.observation: Game starts, castles move into position.
Enemy castle fires, damaging player castle.
Player fires rocket, hitting enemy castle.
Player fires rockets in a high arc.

mechanics.observation: Projectiles deal damage upon collision with the enemy castle, updating the health bar.
Castles show visual damage/destruction when hit.

implementation_notes: Use a 2D physics engine for projectile trajectories.
Implement a simple input handler for touch/mouse drag events to calculate angle and power.

uncertainties.question: How is the visual destruction of the castles implemented?
uncertainties.why_it_matters: It looks like dynamic 2D mesh destruction, which is complex to implement in a simple HTML prototype.

uncertainties.question: Do individual units have health?
uncertainties.why_it_matters: A tombstone appears, but it is unclear if units can be targeted individually or if they die when the floor they are on is destroyed.
